DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Geneva Collins, Esq. 415-986-5900 of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP for Defendant Fairmont Hotel.
Hunter Sims, III, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION IN HIS 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant CCSF's demurrer to the second and third causes of action are sustained without leave to amend because they fail to state a cause of action. (CCP 430.10(e).) Plaintiff complains of a dangerous condition of public property and his exclusive remedy is to pursue a claim under Gov't Code 835. (Amended Complaint, par. 6; Brown v. Poway Unified School Dist. (1993) 4 Cal.4th 820, 829; see also Longfellow v. County of San Luis Obispo (1983) 144 Cal.App.3d 379, 383-385.) In addition, plaintiff cannot pursue causes of action two and three per Gov't Code 815.6. The purpose of the predicate statutes (Street and Highways Code 5611 and 5615 and SF Department of Public Works Code 706.3) is to allow CCSF to recoup its costs when it makes repairs. Any benefit to plaintiff is incidental. (See Haggis v. City of Los Angeles (2000) 22 Cal.4th 490; 499; Schaefer v. Lenahan (1944) 63 Cal.App.2d 324, 327, 332.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)
